items.name: Genji Glove

route.why: Refuse Banon's plea three times, then accept — the reward changes

items.notes: Say no to Banon three times before agreeing and the chest by the bed holds a Genji Glove instead of a Gauntlet.

items.name: Chainsaw (Edgar)

route.why: Set the clock (6:10:50) while you're here for the story

items.notes: Every townsperson lies about the time. Zozo stays reachable in the World of Ruin.

items.name: The delayed-chest trick
items.notes: Several early chests upgrade their contents if left unopened until the World of Ruin (e.g. South Figaro basement, Narshe cliff chests). Patience pays in Elixirs and top-tier gear.

items.name: Lone Wolf: Gold Hairpin or Mog
items.notes: The thief holds Mog hostage on the cliff. Wait and choose: Gold Hairpin (item) or Mog (joins now). Mog can still be recruited in the World of Ruin either way.

items.name: The Emperor's banquet

route.why: Answer well — rewards scale with your score

items.notes: Talk to every soldier, answer diplomatically, take exactly the right pauses. Top scores earn Tintinabar and charm gear.

items.name: WAIT FOR SHADOW

route.why: Let the escape timer run to ~5 seconds — jump early and Shadow dies forever

items.notes: At the airship ledge, choose to wait until Shadow arrives. Leaving without him removes him — and everything downstream of him — from the rest of the game.

items.name: Keep Cid alive

route.why: Feed him only the lively fast fish

items.notes: Catch the fast-swimming fish; slow ones make him worse. Whether he lives changes how Celes leaves the island.

items.name: Terra rejoins

route.why: Return with the Falcon and finish the Humbaba fight

items.notes: She stays with the children until Humbaba is driven off for good.

items.name: Mog

route.why: First stop in ruined Narshe — he unlocks Umaro too

items.notes: Alone in the old moogle den. Bring him along for the cliffs.

items.name: Umaro
items.notes: Drop through the hole behind the esper, take the bone carving, and let Mog do the talking.

items.name: Cyan

route.why: Follow the Crimson Robbers from Nikeah up the mountain

items.notes: His cliffside letters to Lola in Maranda are the trailhead.

items.name: Shadow

route.why: Rescue him in the cave, then bet the Striker at the Colosseum

items.notes: Only exists if you waited on the Floating Continent. If you didn't, Relm is found in the cave instead.

items.name: Gau

route.why: Leap with a three-member party while you're on the Veldt

items.notes: He returns on the next encounter after the Leap. His father's house afterward is worth the detour.

items.name: Relm

route.why: The painting gallery — bring Lightning-proof gear

items.notes: Read the diary in Jidoor, fight through the gallery, defeat Chadarnook.

items.name: Strago

route.why: He only snaps out of the cult trance if Relm is in the party

items.notes: Walk up with Relm and he rejoins on the spot.

items.name: Locke

route.why: Two-party dungeon — he's at the heart of it

items.notes: Bring two capable teams. He hands over the cracked magicite on the way out.

items.name: Gogo
items.notes: Let the Zone Eater swallow the party, survive the crushing-ceiling gauntlet, and the mimic joins.

items.name: Tritoch

route.why: The frozen esper answers now — and opens the path to Umaro

items.notes: Fight it on the cliff where the game began.

items.name: Ragnarok: esper or sword
items.notes: Permanent choice. The esper teaches Ultima; the sword bets into the Lightbringer at the Colosseum. You cannot have both in one playthrough.

items.name: Cursed Shield
items.notes: 256 battles while equipped (with a Ribbon on) uncurses it into the Paladin Shield.

items.name: Odin

route.why: Beneath Figaro's tunnels — and read the queen's story

items.notes: The petrified king. A hidden switch by the throne room later upgrades him to Raiden — which permanently replaces Odin and his Meteor lesson.

items.name: Phoenix
items.notes: The cracked magicite from Locke's hunt — Rasp aside, its revival magic is unique.

items.name: Alexander
items.notes: Sleep at Doma with Cyan in the party, survive the Dream Stooges and the train, free his soul.

items.name: Bahamut
items.notes: Deathgaze ambushes the Falcon at random; it flees and keeps its wounds between fights. Finish it to claim Bahamut.

items.name: The Eight Legendary Dragons

route.why: Six are reachable before the tower — save Gold and Skull for the assault

items.notes: Ice — Narshe cliffs; Storm — Mt. Zozo; Earth — Opera House stage; Red — Phoenix Cave; Blue — Ancient Castle; White — Fanatics' Tower; Gold & Skull — Kefka's Tower.

items.name: Crusader
items.notes: Merton is chaos incarnate — but the magicite's real prize is teaching it at all.

items.name: Bum Rush (Sabin)
items.notes: Duncan lives. Visit with Sabin for his final blitz.

items.name: Hidon & Grand Delta

route.why: Strago's rival — feed the hungry chest coral until the path opens

items.notes: Bring Strago (Relm along is even better). Teaches his ultimate lore.

items.name: Colosseum wagers
items.notes: Marquee bets: Striker → Shadow's duel, Ragnarok sword → Lightbringer, Elixir chains for rare gear. Every wager is a one-item gamble — save first.
